track: ML-8A LightGBM window descriptor v1 (SEAD baseline)
method: LightGBM (window descriptor v1) vs Isolation Forest baseline; separate retrained LSTM-AE bundle
key_finding: LightGBM window descriptor v1 failed to beat the existing Isolation Forest baseline on SEAD (5-seed AUPRC 0.349 vs 0.385) and no LightGBM+decision combination showed onset-recall superiority at the same FA budget, so Gate B and Gate C both failed; ML-8A LightGBM is not a production candidate. On the ALFA fixed-recipe protocol, existing IF+CUSUM continued to pass Gate C (0.625 recall / 7.91 FA/hr advisory).
notes: ALFA fixed-recipe AUPRC comparison: LightGBM 0.843 < IF 0.858 < LSTM-AE 0.872 (LSTM-AE retrained separately as ml8a_retrained_lstm_ae bundle, existing artifacts not overwritten). Final results use full_matrix_gapfix/ artifacts (max_gap_s=2 exposure calc); earlier full_matrix/ runs were superseded because they counted an 850,000s ALFA telemetry gap as normal exposure. ADR includes a table of methodology controls deliberately NOT done post-result (no feature tuning, no Optuna, no threshold changes, holdout stays closed).

track: ML-9 SEAD category residuals (EKF innovations + actuator symmetry)
method: Isolation Forest with added residual features (module-based scoring); CUSUM/K-of-N/Threshold decision layers unchanged from ML-8A
key_finding: Adding EKF innovation and actuator-symmetry residual features gave small category-level recall gains (Position.Z CUSUM/advisory 0.074->0.096; Actuator Outputs+Controls 0.180->0.205) but did not jointly satisfy the pre-registered >=0.05 gain AND >=3/5-seed stability rule, so Gate B failed. ml9_fusion missed operational targets (0.222/25.83 advisory, 0.139/14.49 critical), so Gate C failed.
notes: Frozen manifest had 611 processable flights and 131 blind holdout (differs from the plan's stated 412/76); SHA-256 0e3047b3abc11d09bc5b2b94e35cca117efce8fa49fdc37393c146550b8d0f0d unchanged, holdout untouched. Rare Battery/Vibration/Magnetometer subtypes not modeled. Artifact: artifacts/ml9/uav_sead/full_matrix/, manifest covers 65 file checksums.

track: ML-10 Chronos zero-shot forecaster fusion (SEAD)
method: amazon/chronos-bolt-tiny zero-shot forecaster; max-fusion with ML-9 modules; threshold/K-of-N/CUSUM decision layers reused
key_finding: Chronos-bolt-tiny zero-shot forecasting substantially improved the mechanical/motor category (chronos_motor CUSUM/advisory recall 0.205->0.390, +0.185, 4/5 seeds; critical +0.112, 4/5 seeds), passing Gate B for that category only. The vertical/Position.Z category regressed (0.096->0.023), and combined ml10_fusion still failed Gate C (0.213/23.92 advisory, 0.133/12.83 critical) — chronos_motor not added to default/production fusion, blind holdout stays closed.
notes: Chronos ran CPU-only, 9.3ms average warm inference; 8-flight feasibility projected a 480-flight full pass at 102.4s (actual 101.2s), under the <3hr planning rule. True zero-shot, no fine-tuning/gradient/optimizer steps. Score fusion centralized in src/ml/evaluation/score_fusion.py. Artifact: artifacts/ml10/uav_sead/full_matrix/.

track: ML-11 read-only visualization/exploration phase
method: PCA/t-SNE projections, Spearman correlation, single-feature x category AUC matrix, diagnostic visualizations from existing artifact models
key_finding: Pure read-only visualization/exploration phase across three datasets; no model/threshold/split/scaler/CUSUM artifact was changed, and the 131-flight SEAD blind holdout entered no figure or statistic. Single-feature AUCs are exploratory only, not reported as an evaluation result.
notes: Decisions: (1) +/-10 IQR visual clipping applied only to PCA/t-SNE figures (not to any score/model), disclosed in figure titles. (2) UMAP/seaborn NOT installed (numpy downgrade risk). (3) train_lstm_autoencoder now returns per-epoch train/val loss history logged via src/ml/training_log.py — every model trained from now on leaves a trace. (4) The 'D.1' ~32-session estimate corrected to 64 (dev: 49) sessions.

track: ML-12 ince modul (thin single-feature module) - itki_komutu
method: Single-feature Isolation Forest module on itki_komutu (actuator_thrust_cmd); 3-feature itki_kontrol_ince variant; max-fusion ml12_fusion_itki
thresholds_or_targets: advisory >=0.50 recall @ <=12 FA/hr (stated explicitly for ml12_fusion_itki)
key_finding: Single-feature itki_komutu became the new best-known score for the Actuator Outputs+Controls category, beating both motor_simetrisi (0.205->0.459 CUSUM/advisory) and chronos_motor (0.390->0.459), passing both Gate B1 (gate-determining) and B2 (best-known). But max-fusion with this module (ml12_fusion_itki) only reached 0.217 recall/23.74 FA-hr advisory vs the 0.50 target — the thin module is a strong category specialist that leaves 38.1 FA/hr on normal flights, so max-fusion cannot use it within budget. Gate C failed.
notes: 3-feature itki_kontrol_ince variant underperformed the single-feature version everywhere, confirming dilution scales even at 3 features. Artifact: artifacts/ml12/uav_sead/full_matrix/. Module not added to production fusion; a category-specific alarm channel architecture flagged as a possible future pre-registered phase (became ADR-013).

track: ML-13 two-channel alarm architecture (system + mechanical)
method: Dual-channel boolean OR fusion: system channel = frozen ML-9 existing_fusion, mechanical channel = frozen ML-12 itki_komutu split models

metrics_table.score_or_module: dengeli combined channel, budget-inside advisory row
mekanik kanal alone (Actuator Outputs+Controls), dengeli split
mekanik kanal alone (Actuator Outputs+Controls), esit split

thresholds_or_targets: advisory >=0.50 recall @ <=12 FA/hr; critical >=0.30 recall @ <=2 FA/hr; FA-hour brake of 1.10x pre-registered budget
key_finding: Combining a system channel (ML-9 existing_fusion) and mechanical channel (ML-12 itki_komutu) via boolean OR gave consistent recall gains over the best single channel (e.g. CUSUM/advisory 0.217->0.291, 5/5 seeds) but all gains pushed FA-hour 1.89x-3.83x over the pre-registered 1.10x brake, so Gate B failed on cost. Neither the general operational target (Gate C1) nor a narrower 'mechanical-specific monitor' claim (Gate C2) was met — the mechanical channel alone got closest (K-of-N/advisory 0.541 recall but 12.60 FA-hr, just over budget).
notes: Architecture not moved to production, blind holdout not opened, budget split/policy/OR semantics not changed post-result. tests/test_ml13.py 8/8 passed; wide test suite (excl. MinIO) 186 passed/22 deselected. Artifact: artifacts/ml13/uav_sead/full_matrix/.

track: RFLY-0/RFLY-1 RflyMAD motor/sensor faults (+ pooled SEAD+RFLY) - later corrected by interval-truth audit
method: itki_komutu rule scorer + IF modules; threshold/CUSUM decision layers; two-pass CUSUM Gold construction

metrics_table.score_or_module: itki_komutu (RFLY-only, ORIGINAL whole-flight proxy label - superseded)
itki_komutu (RFLY-only, ORIGINAL whole-flight proxy label - superseded)

thresholds_or_targets: critical >=0.30 recall @ <=2 FA/hr; advisory >=0.50 recall @ <=12 FA/hr (explicit in correction note)
key_finding: CORRECTION FOLDED IN: the original ADR-014 RFLY-only result (itki_komutu threshold/critical 0.573 recall/1.23 FA-hr, originally passing Gate R-C) used a whole-flight anomaly proxy label that is easier than SEAD's event-onset task and overstates performance. The RFLY-1 interval-truth audit (true fault_onset_s/fault_end_s from the ULog rfly_ctrl_lxl message) corrected this: RFLY-only best row dropped to CUSUM/advisory 0.526 recall/22.28 FA-hr, and Gate R-C now FAILS for both RFLY-only and pooled SEAD+RFLY. No row meets the critical/advisory targets after correction; original numbers retained only for audit history, not product-claim evidence.
notes: 5 folder-labeled motor-fault flights had internal control message rfly_ctrl_lxl_no_active_fault contradicting the folder label; excluded as ambiguous (4 from dev/test, 1 from RFLY final holdout, which stayed unopened). Real RflyMAD pool fixed at 490 flights; official first quota amended from 30/30 to 12/12 normal val/test + 27 normal train (R1 amendment) because the normal ceiling was only 51. Pooled experiment fixed a 'ghost imputation' bug (missing-column cross-source median fill) before RFLY-1. Artifacts: artifacts/rfly0/rflymad/official_full_interval_excluding_invalid, artifacts/rfly0/pooled_sead_rfly/official_full_interval_excluding_invalid, artifacts/rfly1/interval_truth_report.json.

track: ML-15 drift calibration layer (session-jackknife)
method: Session-jackknife drift multiplier wrapping Threshold/K-of-N/CUSUM policies (drift_calibration.py)
key_finding: A pure calibration wrapper (session-jackknife drift multiplier) was added around existing Threshold/K-of-N/CUSUM policies without rewriting the decision layer; unit tests confirmed determinism/floor/cap/fallback/policy-identity behavior. Only a single split_00 smoke run was completed (~45 min); the full 5-seed matrix was not started this session due to cost (session-jackknife + CUSUM bootstrap repeated per decision/budget/score).
notes: Parameters q=0.75, floor=1.0, cap=5.0, min_sessions=4 unchanged. Full 5-seed matrix and Gate B/C results completed later in ADR-020.

track: ML-16 Kol L - SEAD LSTM Autoencoder retrain on current splits
method: LSTM Autoencoder (src/ml/models/lstm_autoencoder.py, unchanged architecture), retrained on updated split_manifest.json; wired to official ml14/ml15 fusion+decision pipeline
thresholds_or_targets: critical >=0.30 recall @ <=2 FA/hr; advisory >=0.50 recall @ <=12 FA/hr
key_finding: LSTM-AE retrain raised raw recall (e.g. CUSUM/advisory 0.251 vs existing best 0.126) but FA-hour rose proportionally and no cell stayed within budget, so Gate B failed. CRITICAL HONESTY FINDING: a cross-model consistency check found independently-trained Dense-AE and USAD agents produced IDENTICAL category-level detected/false-alarm counts to LSTM-AE at split_00 threshold/critical. Root-cause diagnosis found the trained model's score ranking correlates with an untrained (randomly initialized) same-architecture model at Spearman rho=0.964, and with a pure ||x||^2 raw-amplitude baseline at rho=0.965 - training adds almost nothing beyond 'how large is the input.' The project-wide unclipped RobustScaler lets a handful of extreme-amplitude windows (real GPS-spoofing jumps AND at least one 'normal'-labeled flight with frozen-GPS/eph~25000 sentinel values) dominate reconstruction error regardless of architecture, so the LSTM recall numbers cannot be presented as 'the LSTM learned a temporal signal.'
notes: ThresholdPolicy separately verified NOT degenerate (only 94/904 test flights alarmed, only 4.3% coincide with window-completion moment) - the artifact is in scaling/feature selection, not the decision layer. Per methodology discipline, scaling was NOT changed post-result in this run. docs/ML_YETERSIZLIKLER_KAYDI.md C.1 updated; new open item B.5 (magnitude domination) logged. Artifact: artifacts/ml_lstm_sead/uav_sead/full_matrix/; tests/test_lstm_sead_integration.py 13/13 passed.

track: ML-16 Kol D - SEAD Dense Autoencoder
method: Dense Autoencoder (src/ml/models/dense_autoencoder.py, new flat/feed-forward architecture), same pipeline/windowing as ADR-016
key_finding: Dense-AE showed the same pattern as LSTM-AE in ADR-016: raw recall exceeded the existing best (e.g. threshold/critical 0.215 vs 0.043) but no cell stayed within budget. The ADR-016 magnitude-domination honesty finding applies unchanged/shared to this run (not re-derived independently) - the mechanism is architecture-independent (unclipped project-wide RobustScaler + a handful of extreme-amplitude windows/sentinel values), so dense_ae_recon recall figures cannot be presented as 'Dense-AE learned signal.'
notes: Gate A intermediate columns matched the frozen ml14 CSV at max_abs_diff=7.1e-15 across 90/90 rows. docs/ML_YETERSIZLIKLER_KAYDI.md C.1 updated; B.5 referenced, not reopened. Artifact: artifacts/ml_dense_ae_sead/uav_sead/full_matrix/; tests/test_dense_ae_sead_integration.py passed.

track: ML-16 Kol U - SEAD USAD (adversarial dual-autoencoder)
method: USAD (shared encoder E + two decoders D1/D2, published two-phase adversarial loss), same pipeline/windowing as ADR-016/017
key_finding: USAD showed the same pattern as LSTM-AE/Dense-AE: raw recall exceeded existing best (threshold/critical 0.217 vs 0.043) but no cell within budget, and the same magnitude-domination finding applies - USAD's adversarial training does not overcome the unclipped RobustScaler's extreme-amplitude window dominance either. Combined result across ADR-016/017/018: all three deep architectures (LSTM-AE, Dense-AE, USAD) tested on SEAD failed the operational target and shared the same amplitude-domination artifact; architecture choice is not discriminating with this data/scaling. Next step should be clipped/robust scaling or an amplitude-normalized score, not retesting these three architectures under the same scaling.
notes: Gate A intermediate columns matched the frozen ml14 CSV at max_abs_diff=7.1e-15, 90/90 rows. docs/ML_YETERSIZLIKLER_KAYDI.md C.1 updated; B.5 referenced. Artifact: artifacts/ml_usad_sead/uav_sead/full_matrix/; tests/test_usad_sead_integration.py passed.

track: ML-16 Kol N - magnitude-normalized scoring experiment (no retraining)
method: Two new score derivations from existing frozen LSTM-AE/Dense-AE/USAD checkpoints (no retrain): (a) relative error |x-x_hat|/(|x|+eps) per channel, (b) per-channel percentile-rank (rankpct)
key_finding: Re-scoring the three frozen SEAD models without retraining, using relative-error and per-channel-percentile scores, tested whether magnitude domination could be fixed at the scoring layer. rankpct barely reduced magnitude dependence (Spearman rho stayed ~0.7-0.9 vs the raw-score baseline ~0.96-0.97 from ADR-016) and kept some recall, but all cells still exceeded budget. relerr genuinely broke magnitude dependence (rho dropped to ~0.15-0.55, sometimes negative) but recall nearly vanished (most critical-recall cells <0.06). This CONFIRMS the ADR-016/017/018 hypothesis that most of the raw recall gain was a magnitude artifact - once magnitude is truly removed, no operationally useful signal remains underneath; the problem is weak underlying learned signal, not architecture choice.
notes: Full rho table (5-seed avg, artifacts/ml16_kol_n/summary.json): LSTM rankpct trained-vs-random rho ~0.81, trained-vs-magnitude ~0.79; LSTM relerr ~0.15/~-0.09; Dense-AE rankpct ~0.83/~0.83; Dense-AE relerr ~0.55/~0.23; USAD rankpct ~0.72/~0.80; USAD relerr ~0.37/~0.13 (baseline raw-score rho ~0.96-0.97 all three, per ADR-016). docs/ML_YETERSIZLIKLER_KAYDI.md B.5 updated but not closed - reframed as 'clipping/normalizing the score alone does not fix it, the underlying signal is already weak.'

track: ML-15 session-jackknife drift calibration, full 5-seed matrix
method: Parallelized session-jackknife drift calibration wrapper (joblib) around Threshold/K-of-N/CUSUM policies; algorithm/thresholds/budgets unchanged from ADR-015
thresholds_or_targets: critical >=0.30 recall @ <=2 FA/hr; advisory >=0.50 recall @ <=12 FA/hr
key_finding: Parallelizing the session-jackknife drift calibration (2.63x speedup, bit-identical to sequential results) allowed the full 5-seed matrix to run (95.72 min vs the earlier ~45 min single-split smoke). Gate B failed because only 2/4 CUSUM cells met the pre-registered stability rule (existing_fusion passed both advisory and critical; itki_komutu passed neither on the required seed-count). Gate C failed because no drift-corrected cell met operational targets - best budget-inside advisory was ml14_fusion CUSUM at 0.1145 recall/8.41 FA-hr, far short of 0.50. Drift correction pulled some cells' FA within budget but did not close the recall gap.
notes: Drift multiplier across 90 fits ranged min/median/max = 1.0/1.0/5.0; CUSUM advisory median multiplier 1.331623. Blind 200-flight holdout untouched. Manifest 59 files SHA-256 reverified. Artifact: artifacts/ml15/uav_sead/full_matrix/.

track: ADSB-0 - new ADS-B physics-consistency residual track (segmentation + physics infra)
method: Arithmetic physical-consistency residuals (reported channel vs derived-from-raw-lat/lon/alt counterpart); no learned model
dataset: adsb.lol/readsb ADS-B telemetry (real-data access blocked this session; synthetic data used for dev/test)
key_finding: After 9 methods (IF, LightGBM, Chronos, LSTM-AE, Dense-AE, USAD, magnitude-normalized score, drift-calibrated fusion) all failed Gate C on SEAD/RFLY with root cause traced to a small/heterogeneous normal pool plus magnitude-domination artifact, a new parallel track was started: arithmetic physics-consistency residuals on ADS-B telemetry instead of a learned model, a design where magnitude-domination cannot structurally arise. Segmentation, physics-residual, and injection code was written and unit-tested entirely on synthetic data (329 passed, 6 skipped); real ADS-B data access was blocked this session (MinIO/Docker unavailable on this machine; real parsed data exists only on a teammate's machine).
notes: EARTH_RADIUS_M constant mismatch (WGS84 111320 m/deg vs haversine 6371km) initially created a spurious systematic residual in tests, fixed. Named ADSB-0/ADSB-1 to avoid namespace collision with ML-N/RFLY-N, entirely new src/adsb/ package. Not yet written: visualization gallery, real-data validation of new_leg_agreement rate, ADSB-0 phase gate closure. Plans: docs/ADSB0_INGEST_SEGMENT_PLAN.md, docs/ADSB1_PHYSICS_DETECTOR_PLAN.md (ADSB-1 target: recall>=0.70 @ FA<=0.05, pre-registered).

track: ADS-B reset + first real 3-day model round (Dense-AE/LSTM-AE/USAD/LSTM-forecaster)
method: Dense-AE, LSTM-AE, USAD, LSTM-forecaster trained on real ADS-B data; magnitude_domination_check diagnostic
dataset: Real ADS-B (adsb.lol), 3 days: v2026.02.28, v2026.03.01, v2026.03.16 (~3GB each, 256,150,550 rows total, 638 Silver parts)
key_finding: First real-data training round found LSTM-AE (rho=0.919), USAD (rho=0.986), and LSTM-forecaster (rho=0.890) all magnitude-flagged; only Dense-AE (rho=0.086) was clean - root cause was completely unscaled features (alt in thousands, vertical_rate_ms single-digit). After adding ClippedRobustScaler (clip=5.0, train-only fit) and gradient clipping, a second round flagged Dense-AE instead (rho=0.81/0.83) while LSTM-AE became borderline-clean (rho=0.77/0.80) and USAD's rho dropped (~-0.05) but its loss STILL numerically exploded (23 billion) - meaning the rho diagnostic alone is insufficient; training-stability must be separately monitored (not yet added). Synthetic-corruption validation showed Dense-AE/LSTM-AE/LSTM-forecaster detected corrupt>clean in 5/5 scenarios but effect size varied hugely (ground_speed_biased strong 2.3x-10.75x; position_ramp/track/altitude_dropout weak 1.01x-1.17x, practically indistinguishable); USAD scored 0/5 due to exploded training.
notes: Two independent, uncoordinated efforts (src/adsb/ by Claude, src/adsb_behavioral/ by Codex) both attempted the same problem; Codex's version got 97.6% synthetic recall but 25.54 false-alarms/hour on natural data (unusable). Both archived to archive/2026-07-10_{legacy_non_adsb_ml,rejected_adsb_attempts}/; old ML-0..16/RFLY track also archived. New unified adsb/ (root-level) track started. Only 10/638 Silver parts (3000 aircraft) used, not full volume. Blind holdout not yet defined.

track: Persistent synthetic test/validation corpus + parser inventory + shuffle bug fix
method: adsb/synthetic.py::save_synthetic_batch persisted synthetic corpus generation using PHYSICS_BREAK_RECIPES; parser inventory audit
dataset: Real ADS-B Silver (60 parts, 22,520,807 rows, 18,000 aircraft) -> synthetic corpus
key_finding: Confirmed a single canonical historical adsb.lol tar parser (parse_trace_bytes) is used consistently across both independent attempts (current and archived Codex version) with identical unit conversions - no inconsistency found. Built a persistent, reusable synthetic corpus (8,910 val flights x clean+5 recipes, 4,467,115 rows each, 765MB total) replacing a previous in-memory-only, throwaway synthetic generation. Found and fixed a real bug: np.random.Generator.shuffle() silently mishandled pandas ArrowStringArray from .unique() - harmless at small scale (3 parts, 2440 flights, tested) but unverified at scale; fixed by converting to plain numpy object arrays in both the synthetic-dataset script and the real training script's train/val split.
notes: Design changed from one-file-per-flight-per-recipe (~59,000 tiny files, too slow on Windows) to one consolidated file per recipe. Open item: seg[seg['flight_id']==fid] full-table scan per flight took ~25 min for 8910 flights; needs seg.groupby('flight_id') at full 638-part scale to avoid super-linear blowup.

track: First full training round (60 parts) + z-score confidence score + literature review
method: Dense-AE, LSTM-AE, LSTM-forecaster (USAD excluded, still numerically unstable); masked_mse; z_score_confidence
dataset: Real ADS-B (60 Silver parts, train 39,555 flights); synthetic corpus (5 injection scenarios) for AUC evaluation
key_finding: Added altitude_source_residual (barometric vs geometric altitude divergence, motivated by literature on self-consistency checks for ADS-B spoofing detection) and a z-score confidence layer. First full 60-part training round: all three trained models were magnitude-domination FLAGGED with pooled AUCs only 0.552-0.572, barely above chance on most scenarios (ground_speed_biased AUC 0.65-0.74; track_frozen/position_ramp_stealthy ~0.51-0.55; altitude_dropout 0.48-0.50, below random in two models). Root cause diagnosed experimentally: masked_mse weights all 8 channels equally, but raw channels (alt, ground_speed_ms, track_deg) have large structured/easy-to-learn variance while residual channels are near-noise in normal flight - equal-weighted loss lets optimization focus on easy/large channels and ignore residual channels, explaining both magnitude-domination and low AUC via one mechanism.
notes: altitude_dropout's below-random AUC suspected to be a measurement artifact from masked_mse's denominator shrinking under NaN-masking, not a real inverse signal - unconfirmed, open item. Found and fixed a memory-crash bug (LSTM scoring 2.85M windows in one forward pass wanted ~20.8GB) via _score_batched(); BATCH_SIZE 64->512 cut Dense-AE training from ~36min to ~2.4min estimate. Open items: weighted-loss experiment (3-5x on residual channels) not yet run; USAD still excluded; category-conditional normality noted but untried; S2 rule-channel (squawk/nic/nac_p/sil) not yet written. Plots: artifacts/adsb/plots/.

track: Rule-based penalty scorer (ResidualRuleScorer) - beats NNs + AUC-ceiling finding
method: ResidualRuleScorer: per-channel robust z-score (train-normal median/MAD), pen_c=min(max(0,z_c-3),10), uniform weight sum, window-mean score
dataset: Synthetic corpus (5 injection recipes) for AUC comparison against NN results from ADR-024
key_finding: A simple rule-based penalty scorer (per-channel robust z-score, no learning) beat all three NN architectures on pooled synthetic AUC: 0.600 vs 0.552-0.572 for Dense-AE/LSTM-AE/LSTM-forecaster (ADR-024), especially on track_frozen (0.679 vs best NN 0.523, +0.16). CRITICAL EVALUATION FINDING: timeline plots revealed pre-onset windows of corrupted flights were being labeled anomalous via a whole-file proxy label, capping the best-possible AUC at ~0.75 regardless of detector quality - the same 'whole-flight proxy -> interval truth' bug RFLY-1 found and fixed (ADR-014 correction). Under this proxy label, ground_speed_biased (0.727) and track_frozen (0.679) are effectively near their measurement ceiling.
notes: Two-round tuning: round 1 MAD-floor default made altitude_source_residual (MAD=0, 25ft quantization) a hair-trigger (93.8% of normal windows got nonzero penalty, AUC 0.582); round 2 generalized rule (MAD-exactly-0 channels excluded entirely, not floored) gave the 0.600 pooled AUC. Weights uniform w=1, pre-registered, not tuned post-result. Open items: window-label correction + re-measurement (pre-registered label-only fix), CUSUM accumulation for stealthy ramp, full-volume (638 parts + new v2025.06.15 tar), NN weighted-loss experiment still not run.

track: Immutable ADS-B run manifest and provenance contract
method: adsb/run_manifest.py - fail-if-exists run_dir, byte/SHA-256/parquet-footer+schema hashing, split hashes, config/git dirty-state, synthetic-path/flight-ID leakage guard
key_finding: Added an immutable run-manifest/provenance system so every ADS-B run is independently hash-verified and auditable; 14/14 new tests passed (9 manifest + 5 evaluation). Found an unresolved row-count discrepancy between manifest provenance footer measurement (256,155,009) and the previously documented total (256,150,550), a +4,459 row gap deliberately recorded as unresolved_do_not_silently_correct rather than silently reconciled.
notes: CLI does not auto-discover inputs and carries no holdout/Downloads default. Concrete scoring runs will get their own manifests once frozen config/splits are known; holdout pool not accessed or frozen at this step.

track: Synthetic interval truth v2 and separate immutable corpus
method: adsb/truth.py - row-level injection_active/observable_changed/evaluable_truth separated from event interval; primary label q_w>0
dataset: Synthetic corpus v2 (8,910 flights x clean+5 scenarios)
key_finding: Built interval-truth v2 to fix the same whole-flight-proxy labeling problem flagged in ADR-025 - row-level truth now distinguishes whether an injection is active, whether it is actually observable, and whether it is evaluable, rather than labeling entire post-onset windows anomalous by file. Full corpus (8,910 flights x 6 variants, 26,802,690 rows, 646,160,578 bytes) written fail-if-exists with SHA-256/footer per file; full ADS-B regression 161/161 passed. Of 666,739 active dropout rows, only 570,666 are observable-changed, confirming a naive onset-to-end proxy would have overcounted.
notes: V1 files unchanged/untouched. An earlier incomplete synthetic namespace (adsb_v2/) was explicitly marked INCOMPLETE_DO_NOT_USE.md rather than overwritten. Frozen-rule corrected-truth scoring deferred to 'Step 3'; v2 corpus will never be used for fit/train/calibration.

track: Frozen rule scorer re-scored on corrected truth-v2 (Step 3)
method: ADR-025's ResidualRuleScorer (unchanged calibration/threshold) re-scored on truth-v2 row-level labels

metrics_table.score_or_module: rule scorer - stealthy ramp event recall

key_finding: Re-scoring the frozen rule scorer on corrected truth-v2 labels (no calibration/threshold changes) gave pooled AUROC/AUPRC 0.764883/0.883313 - well above the ~0.75 proxy-label ceiling identified in ADR-025, confirming the label fix mattered. Ground-speed and track recipes have strong event recall (0.96/0.95) with delays of 19s/57s; the stealthy ramp recipe has high nominal event recall (0.80) but very low active-interval micro-coverage (0.18), meaning detection mostly happens late/briefly relative to the full injected interval. Clean natural-reference rate is 4.808533 episode/hour scoreable, with 89.2% of flights showing at least one alarm at this frozen threshold.
notes: No pre-existing NN checkpoint available for a corrected re-score; the old (label-bugged) NN JSON was kept only as historical_label_bugged_no_checkpoint_rescore, no corrected NN claim made. Artifact: artifacts/adsb/runs/20260713_step3_corrected_rule_v1/, regression 18/18 passed. The 0.95 historical score threshold used is NOT an operationally-approved threshold; that comes later with CUSUM in Step 5.

track: Two-axis velocity residual and causal Page-CUSUM core
method: East/north velocity residuals (reported vs derived-from-consecutive-position); per-axis positive/negative Page CUSUM state (adsb/cusum.py)
thresholds_or_targets: 2 m/s vector target, converted to a 2/sqrt(2) per-axis lower bound
key_finding: Built a causal two-axis Page-CUSUM detector for the previously-weak stealthy position-ramp scenario (ADR-025 flagged this as invisible without accumulation), with strict flight/ground/gap/time reset semantics and prefix-causality. The numerical CUSUM threshold h is deliberately NOT chosen by the core itself - it must be derived from frozen natural-burden calibration in a later step, kept separate from any synthetic-recall-driven selection.
notes: 30/30 tests passed (test_adsb_cusum.py + test_adsb_features.py). MAD=0 channel excluded without floor. If one axis is missing, serialization reports axis_coverage_status=degraded_axis_coverage, treated as a gate blocker not a pass in later steps. CUSUM channels not yet added to NN PRIMARY_FEATURES; no new NN training done.

track: Full-volume streaming baseline (rule+CUSUM natural burden across all 638 parts)
method: Rule scorer + CUSUM h=1 streamed across full real ADS-B volume
dataset: Real ADS-B, 638 Silver parts, 256,155,009 rows: 2026-02-28 (fit/calibration), 2026-03-01 (development), 2026-03-16 (frozen rehearsal)
thresholds_or_targets: engineering-advisory assumption of 12 episode/hour (explicitly NOT an operationally measured requirement)
key_finding: Ran the rule+CUSUM(h=1) pipeline across the full 638-part / 256.16M-row real dataset successfully (10,588s) but declined to freeze it as the main configuration: CUSUM h=1 natural alarm saturation is too high (99.1-99.2% of scoreable flights alarm at least once; evaluable-row alarm rate ~78-80%), meaning episode-level merging masks a saturated per-row alarm rate. A ~2.1-2.5x burden difference between fast and slow cadence strata is also hidden by episode merging. A correctness-invariant violation was found and audited (V1 raw moving-block p95 5.745749/hr was lower than the observed full-flight rate 6.071336/hr); a conservative upper=max(observed, raw p95) audit revised 9/18 candidates upward but the h=1 selection itself did not change.
notes: The 12 episode/hour budget is a pre-registered engineering-advisory assumption for this round, not a measured operational requirement - meeting it alone would not have been sufficient for a pass. East/north MADs 1.736830/1.542729, both axes active. Checksums verified (9/9 index-verified). Truth-v2 CUSUM event recall/delay/coverage not yet measured at this ADR. New h/budget selection requires new user-approved pre-registration and new namespace.

track: Full-volume S2 natural reason burden (data-quality channel)
method: S2 declared-status, position-quality, altitude-availability, message-gap channels kept separate from residual penalty/CUSUM
key_finding: Measured natural (non-attack) data-quality 'reason burden' across the full real dataset (542,461 flight segments, 497,570.9 scoreable flight-hours) separately from the physics-residual penalty/CUSUM channel, explicitly not labeled as attack ground-truth or false-positive rate. MESSAGE_GAP is the dominant natural reason at 2.89 episode/hour pooled; NIC-unknown is next at 0.53/hour; all others are below 0.2/hour. Legacy Silver lacks update-age fields, so all rows are freshness_unknown.
notes: Ran on 4 deterministic workers, 638/638 parts, 272.2s. Checksums re-verified (2/2). 54/54 related regression tests passed. Open item: Step 7 corrected-CUSUM truth-v2 evaluation not yet complete; wide regression found 221/222 passing, with the one failure being a frozen adsb/features.py byte-hash mismatch that blocks corrected-CUSUM claims until resolved (fail-closed, not loosened).

track: Step 7 gate - main rule+CUSUM/S2 configuration freeze decision
method: Combined gate evaluation: truth-v2 corrected rule AUROC/AUPRC + event recall, full-volume CUSUM h=1 natural burden, S2 natural burden, three-NN magnitude-domination status, hash-chain integrity
dataset: Real ADS-B full volume + synthetic truth-v2

metrics_table.note: also 5.738076 development, 5.326149 rehearsal; scoreable-flight alarm rate ~0.991

thresholds_or_targets: 12 episode/hour engineering-advisory assumption (not met, saturated); three-NN magnitude-domination FLAGGED threshold
key_finding: The Step 7 gate to freeze the main rule+CUSUM/S2 configuration as the primary ADS-B anomaly detector FAILED on any of three independently sufficient grounds: (1) CUSUM's natural alarm rate is operationally saturated (~99% of flights alarm, evaluable-row alarm rate 78-80%); (2) all three NN architectures remain magnitude-domination FLAGGED (rho ~0.86/0.90, 0.84/0.89, 0.94/0.92) with pooled label-bugged AUCs only 0.552-0.572 and no corrected re-score exists; (3) the corrected-CUSUM truth-v2 comparison is fail-closed blocked because the frozen adsb/features.py byte hash does not match the current checkout hash and could not be resolved as just a line-ending difference, so the hash gate was not loosened and no corrected-CUSUM claim was made from current code.
notes: Because Step 7 failed, Step 8 (Dense-AE 4x treatment, USAD smoke test) was not started, and Step 9 (holdout freeze/unseal) was not reached - the three raw tars remain unopened/unhashed. Next step requires user-approved new pre-registration; no post-hoc substitution for the Step-5 candidate is allowed.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - new candidate infrastructure (phase/cadence-conditional NN)
method: Context-conditional residual forecaster: NN predicts next-step location/scale per residual channel given flight phase (from past vertical-rate only), track sin/cos, real delta-t, cadence; hierarchical conformal calibration
key_finding: Opened a new, separate candidate namespace (contextual_physics_v1) for a phase/cadence-conditional residual forecaster intended to address heterogeneous-normal-distribution issues seen throughout SEAD/RFLY/ADS-B work, without touching or superseding the Step-5/Step-7 rule+CUSUM artifacts. New target test suite passed 21/21; wide regression 242 passed/1 deselected (the known missing frozen features.py hash test from ADR-032). Explicitly stated these numbers are software/smoke evidence only, not a real-data detection metric.
notes: Flight phase is derived causally (no future leakage). Strict scaler excludes MAD=0 channels without floor. Fail-closed tests cover: synthetic fit/calibration contamination, wrong data role, implicit alpha, budget overrun, MAD=0 floor, mixed-channel fusion, future-looking phase, cross-flight window leakage. Total alert-alpha/burden budget and numeric config not yet frozen by the user; open numbers listed in docs/adsb_contextual_candidate_v1_prereg_2026-07-14.md.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - training config frozen pre-result
method: Same contextual residual forecaster; training config frozen before running
dataset: Step-5 fit-role real ADS-B data (237 fit-day parquet parts)
key_finding: Froze the contextual_physics_v1 training config before any training run: deterministic 2% of fit-role flights (seed 20260714) for model/scaler, separate 2% of calibration-role flights (seed 20260715) reserved only for natural magnitude diagnostics. Config: 12-history/1-horizon, lagged 3-row phase, 2/5/15s cadence, 60s max gap, robust clip 5, hidden-32 single-layer LSTM, bounded scale 0.1-5.0, 5 epochs, batch 512, lr 0.001, seed 0, explicit 1.0 loss weight on scaled active channels, no sweep.
notes: Runner verifies Step-5 byte/SHA records for 237 fit-day inputs before use; does not read synthetic/truth-v2/development/rehearsal/holdout. Runner unit/integration smoke tests 3/3, prior contextual targeted suite 21/21 passed. This ADR is not a detection result - training had not yet been executed at this point.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - normal-only training run complete, magnitude gate PASS
method: Context-conditional residual forecaster, trained with weighted Gaussian NLL loss
dataset: Step-5 fit role: 2,929 flights / 1,267,625 rows (deterministic subset of 149,462 fit flights)

metrics_table.score_or_module: contextual_physics_v1 training loss (weighted Gaussian NLL, 5 epochs)

key_finding: Contextual-physics v1 completed its first real normal-only training run (5 epochs, weighted Gaussian NLL loss falling 0.795->0.709) on a deterministic 2,929-flight/1.27M-row subset of Step-5 fit-role flights. The magnitude-domination gate PASSED clearly: trained-vs-untrained Spearman rho=0.6496 and trained-vs-target-magnitude rho=0.6542, both well below the 0.8 flagging threshold and far below the 0.84-0.94 FLAGGED range seen for the three SEAD/ADS-B NNs (ADR-016/017/018/024/032). This is explicitly only a magnitude-gate pass, not a detection/synthetic AUC/recall result - no threshold, calibration, or anomaly benefit has been measured yet.
notes: altitude_source_residual excluded (MAD=0, no floor). Checkpoint has 9,546 parameters, reloaded strict=True. Separate 770-flight natural-calibration diagnostic set (332,510 windows) scored but not fed back into optimizer/threshold. All hashes (checkpoint, training report, run manifest, checksum index) verified 5/5. No synthetic training rows, no threshold sweep, no truth-v2/development/rehearsal/holdout access; code/git unchanged during run.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - training result mathematically interpreted; detection result explicitly separated
method: Documentation/report synthesis, no new computation
key_finding: A comprehensive report (docs/adsb_overall_model_report_2026-07-14.md) documented the contextual model's math/architecture and explicitly stated that the current rho<0.8 magnitude PASS is NOT a detection success and does NOT reverse the Step-7 FAIL decision. Old NN/rule/CUSUM comparisons were pulled from prior ADRs (024/025/028/030/032) for context; no new AUROC/AUPRC/recall was invented for the contextual candidate.
notes: Open item unchanged from ADR-035: conformal calibration, temporal thresholds, development/rehearsal, and truth-v2 evaluation are all blocked until the user freezes a total natural alarm-episode budget and channel shares numerically. Triple holdout pool remains closed.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - alarm budget Pareto grid + channel share + temporal profile pre-registration
method: Pre-registration of budget grid, channel/S2 shares, temporal thresholds (no evaluation run)
thresholds_or_targets: Pareto grid of total budget: [0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0] episode per 100 scoreable flight-hours
key_finding: Closed ADR-036's open item by having the user approve (via AskUserQuestion) three pre-registered decisions: a 5-point Pareto grid instead of a single total-budget number, evidence-weighted channel/S2 shares, and Claude-proposed/user-approved instant/persistence/accumulation temporal profiles. Channel shares were derived from ADR-028's corrected truth-v2 pooled recipe AUROCs (ground-speed 0.974023, track 0.889552, vertical-rate 0.696337) via skill=AUROC-0.5 normalization; deliberately did NOT use the weaker 'ramp' AUROC (0.558927) for the two velocity-residual channels since ADR-029's two-axis CUSUM was built specifically to fix that weak representation - instead the weakest channel's skill (vertical_rate, 0.196337) was used as the floor. S2 got a fixed 15% share (deterministic flag, no AUROC); remaining 85% split proportionally across 5 physics channels, summing exactly to 1.0.
notes: Persistence window set to 30s to avoid worsening track's observed ~57s natural delay. CUSUM h threshold deliberately NOT chosen by this document - per ADR-029 contract it will be derived separately from natural calibration at each Pareto point. User also approved starting a parallel Isolation Forest exploration (ADR-038). Full derivation: docs/adsb_contextual_physics_v1_alarm_budget_prereg_2026-07-14.md, configs/adsb_contextual_physics_v1_alarm_budget.json.

track: Isolation Forest - parallel exploration on residual channels, separate namespace
method: isolation_forest_contextual_v1: IF on same 5 residual channels + StrictNaturalRobustScaler as contextual_physics_v1; score_samples() continuous output
dataset: Real ADS-B, 40/237 fit-role parts subsample (12,003,593 fit rows/26,241 flights; 2,573,412 diagnostic rows/6,561 flights)
key_finding: A parallel Isolation Forest exploration on the same 5 physics-residual channels used by contextual_physics_v1 was written and self-checked on a real-data subsample (26,241 fit flights) the same day. Result: rho_trained_vs_magnitude=0.995958 and, more damningly, rho_trained_vs_shuffled_channel_fit=0.995841 - an IF fit on data with channel structure deliberately destroyed (shuffled) produces nearly the SAME ranking as IF fit on real data, meaning the current flat 5-channel IF is not learning genuine multivariate structure at all, just measuring a Euclidean/Mahalanobis-like magnitude of the scaled residuals - far worse than contextual_physics_v1's own rho (0.65) and worse than even the FLAGGED NN range (0.84-0.94). IF in its current raw form is not supported as a valuable complementary signal and will not be fused or compared further.
notes: IF shares the exact 5 residual channels and scaler as contextual_physics_v1 (verified code reuse); fit() only accepts natural_clean_fit role and raises ValueError at runtime if contains_synthetic=True. Known limitation: unlike the LSTM side's availability mask, IF drops any row with a NaN in the 5 active channels entirely (complete-case), narrower coverage than the contextual model. 6/6 new tests passed. This was explicitly a deliberate subsample exploration, not the full-volume/hash-chained production run. Only plausible next step (untried): phase/cadence-conditional, separate IF fits.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - first real natural-calibration run
method: HierarchicalConformalCalibrator fit on scored calibration-role flights
dataset: Real ADS-B, 60/237 fit-day parts subsample, calibration role (8,182 flights of 37,208 total in split_contract)
key_finding: First real-data run of the calibration step: scored 8,182 calibration-role flights (3,321,776 windows, 16,001,483-row long-format table) and fit the hierarchical conformal calibrator. Grouping health was good for 4/5 channels (19/20 phase x cadence combos directly supported for speed/heading/east/north residuals); vertical_rate was weaker (12/16 direct) but channel-level fallback always available. This is purely a calibration-health report - no alarms produced, no p-value thresholded yet.
notes: Found and fixed two bugs: (1) split_contract flight_ids already carried a date prefix that the calibration script was redundantly re-adding, producing zero matches; (2) repeated the same batching bug pattern from earlier sessions - contextual_channel_scores tried to process 3.3M windows in one forward pass (~29.8GB), fixed with _score_batched() (20,000-row groups). min_group_size=1000 used (10x target minimum p, per ADR-037/Q2). Not a full-volume (237/237) or hash-chained production run.

track: Contextual-physics v1 - first real natural-load (development) measurement
method: adsb_contextual_physics_v1_development_burden.py - scores frozen model on unseen development-day data, measures alarm episode/scoreable-flight-hour at fixed alpha grid via instant/persistence decision profiles

metrics_table.note: at alpha=0.0136, much higher natural burden

thresholds_or_targets: Pareto grid tightest point 0.1 episode/100 scoreable-hours = 0.001/hour (from ADR-037)
key_finding: First real measurement (not a search) of natural alarm load at a fixed alpha grid on a single unseen development day (704 flights, 1 of 216 development parts). 4 of 5 profiles (freeze, bias, inconsistency, and the small-alpha end of spike) had very low natural burden even at the loosest tested alpha (e.g. speed_bias 0.0053 episode/hr) - comfortably meeting even the tightest Pareto point (0.001/hr). vertical_rate_spike behaved very differently, jumping to 4.89 episode/hr even at a fairly tight alpha=0.0136, indicating its conformal p-value distribution is much flatter and needs a much smaller alpha for the same target. This is explicitly too small a sample (single part, 704 flights) to set any operational alpha.
notes: Found two real performance problems: (1) development-day windowing tried to allocate a single 4.56GB array even with 15 parts, fixed via chunked processing; (2) apply_detector_profile's row-by-row .loc-assignment Python loop could not finish even one channel of an 8-alpha x 5-profile sweep on 2.1M rows in 15+ minutes - NOT fixed, only worked around by shrinking scale this run; flagged as a real bottleneck needing a separate performance pass. Known scope narrowing: east/north velocity-residual (stealthy ramp) profiles were not run at all this round; ADR-037's planned joint 2-axis CUSUM integration for those also not done. Rehearsal role and truth-v2 comparison still not started.
